title: PRD: Monocle Layout untuk Sub-Agent Extension
description:
# PRD: Monocle Layout untuk Sub-Agent Extension

## Problem Statement

Tim pengguna memiliki anggota dengan laptop layar kecil (11 inch). Layout tiling dan bottom-stack yang ada saat ini membagi area terminal menjadi pane-pane kecil ketika lebih dari 2 sub-agent dijalankan paralel, menyebabkan setiap pane hanya muat beberapa baris teks dan tidak nyaman digunakan. Layout existing membagi dalam satu window/tab yang sama.

## Solution

Tambahkan layout **monocle** sebagai opsi ketiga. Berbeda dengan tiling (membagi pane dalam 1 window) dan bottom-stack (master di atas, sub-agent horizontal di bawah), monocle membuat window/tab **baru** di session/workspace yang sama untuk setiap tipe agent. Setiap sub-agent dalam tipe yang sama dikelompokkan dalam window yang sama (sebagai pane di dalamnya), memaksimalkan real estate layar.

```
Before (tiling, 3 sub-agents di 11 inch):
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│ main agent          │ sub-a      │
│                     │────────────│
│                     │ sub-b      │
│                     │────────────│
│                     │ sub-c      │
└──────────────────────────────────┘
(setiap pane terlalu kecil)

After (monocle, 3 sub-agents di 11 inch):
Window 0: main agent    Window "scout":    Window "worker":
┌────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐   ┌────────────────┐
│                    │  │ scout-1      │   │ worker-1       │
│                    │  │              │   │                │
│  (full screen)     │  │  (full pane) │   │  (full pane)   │
│                    │  │              │   │                │
│                    │  └──────────────┘   └────────────────┘
└────────────────────┘

Window "reviewer":
┌────────────────┐
│ reviewer-1     │
│                │
│  (full pane)   │
│                │
└────────────────┘
```

## User Stories

1. Sebagai pengguna di laptop 11 inch, saya ingin sub-agent pane menggunakan full screen, sehingga saya bisa membaca output tanpa scroll terus-menerus.

2. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin semua sub-agent dengan tipe yang sama (misal scout) berada dalam satu window/tab, sehingga navigasi antar tipe agent terorganisir.

3. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin window/tab sub-agent tetap berada dalam session/workspace yang sama dengan main agent, sehingga tidak ada kebocoran antar project.

4. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin sub-agent yang di-spawn tetap terisolasi di session/workspace tempat saya bekerja, sehingga ketika saya pindah workspace tidak ada spawn di workspace lain.

5. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin bisa beralih antar layout (tiling, bottom-stack, monocle) melalui `/subagent-config` yang sudah ada, sehingga konsisten dengan UX yang sudah dikenal.

6. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin layout monocle bisa di-set sebagai default di project-level atau global-level config, sesuai preferensi tim.

7. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin nama window/tab hanya menampilkan nama agent (scout, worker, dll), tanpa detail task-nya, sehingga bersih dan mudah dibaca saat navigasi.

8. Sebagai pengguna yang spawning multiple agent dengan tipe sama (misal 2-3 scout), saya ingin mereka semua masuk dalam 1 window/tab yang sama, sehingga tidak membanjiri navigasi.

9. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin pane-pane dalam satu window/tab di-equalize secara proporsional, sehingga tidak ada satu pane yang memonopoli space.

10. Sebagai pengguna, saya ingin ekstensi mendeteksi backend yang active (tmux/herdr) dan menggunakan API yang sesuai untuk window/tab operations.

## Implementation Decisions

### New file: `monocle.ts`

File baru yang terisolasi dari `mux-layout.ts`. Berisi logika monocle murni:

- `createMonocleSurface()` — fungsi utama, menerima parameter backend-agnostic (splitFn, createWindowFn, getWindowPanesFn) + agent name
- `monocleState` — Map<agentType, { windowId, panes[] }> — state internal untuk tracking grouping
- `resetMonocleLayout()` — reset state, dipanggil saat session baru
- `getGroupName(name: string)` — helper untuk extract agent type dari nama (misal "scout-A" → "scout")

### Logic `createMonocleSurface()`:

```
1. Tentukan groupName = agent type dari name parameter
2. Cek monocleState: apakah sudah ada window untuk groupName ini?
   - Belum ada:
     a. Panggil createWindowFn(groupName) → buat window/tab baru
     b. Panggil splitFn(name, ...) → split pane dalam window baru (pakai DEFAULT_SPLIT_RATIO)
     c. Simpan windowId + paneId ke monocleState
   - Udah ada:
     a. Dapatkan windowId dari monocleState
     b. Panggil splitFn(name, ..., fromSurface=lastPane) → split pane baru dalam window yang sama
     c. Panggil getWindowPanesFn(windowId) → dapatkan semua pane dalam window
     d. Equalize heights pane-pane tersebut
     e. Update list panes di monocleState
3. Return paneId dari pane yang baru dibuat
```

### Backend operations di `tmux-mux.ts` dan `herdr-mux.ts`

**tmux-mux.ts** — tambah:
- `tmuxCreateWindow(sessionId: string, windowName: string): string` — `tmux new-window -t <sessionId> -n <windowName> -P -F "#{window_id}"`
- `tmuxGetWindowPanes(windowId: string): string[]` — `tmux list-panes -t <windowId> -F "#{pane_id}"`
- `tmuxGetCurrentSession(): string` — `tmux display-message -p "#{session_id}"`

**herdr-mux.ts** — tambah:
- `herdrCreateTab(workspaceId: string, tabName: string): string` — `herdr tab create --workspace <wsId> --name <tabName>`
- `herdrGetTabPanes(tabId: string): string[]` — `herdr pane layout --tab <tabId>` → parse JSON
- Workspace ID dari `process.env.HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID`

### Perubahan `types.ts`

- `LayoutType` → `"tiling" | "bottom-stack" | "monocle"`

### Perubahan `mux.ts:createSurface()`

- Route: if `layout === "monocle"` → panggil `createMonocleSurface()` dari `./monocle.ts`
- Kirim backend-specific `createWindowFn` dan `getWindowPanesFn` sesuai backend detection

### Perubahan `wizard.ts:editLayout()`

- Tambah opsi: `"monocle — Setiap agent type di window/tab sendiri, full screen"`
- Tampilkan current state dengan ✅

### Config tidak berubah

- `config.ts` sudah generic — layout type validation aja diupdate ke 3 values

### Session/Workspace binding

- **Kritis**: Semua operasi window/tab harus explicit target ke current session/workspace
- tmux: anchor dari `TMUX_PANE` → `display-message -p "#{session_id}"`
- herdr: anchor dari `HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID`
- Tidak ada fallback ke default session — mencegah kebocoran antar workspace

## Testing Decisions

### Filosofi

Test hanya external behavior (layout grouping, equalize, window creation routing). Jangan test internal state monocleState secara langsung — test observable outputnya (window mana yang dibuat, pane mana yang di-return).

### Seam 1: monocle.ts pure logic (highest value)

Mock `splitFn`, `createWindowFn`, `getWindowPanesFn` — pattern identik dengan `mux-layout.test.ts`:

| Test case | Input | Assertion |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| 1st subagent → create new window + split | scout-1 | panggil createWindowFn("scout"), splitFn sekali |
| 2nd subagent same type → reuse window + equalize | scout-2 | splitFn dalam window yg sama, equalize 2 panes |
| 2nd subagent different type → create new window | worker-1 | panggil createWindowFn("worker") baru, splitFn |
| 3rd instansi same type → reuse window + equalize 3 panes | scout-3 | equalize 3 panes dalam window scout |
| resetMonocleLayout() → clear state | - | fresh start, create window baru lagi |
| Pane closure → fallback behavior | split gagal | handle error, reset group state |

### Seam 2: mux.ts dispatch routing (cheap)

| Test case | Input | Assertion |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| layout="monocle" | mock backend, panggil createSurface | panggil createMonocleSurface (via spy) |
| layout="tiling" | same | panggil createTileSurface (regression) |
| layout="bottom-stack" | same | panggil createTileSurface dgn bottom-stack (regression) |

### Seam 3: Backend (skipped unit test)

Tidak ada prior art integration test untuk tmux/herdr backend di project ini. Cukup di-cover manual.

### Prior art

Tes pattern dari `test/mux-layout.test.ts` — mock backend, assert splitCalls & resizeCalls. Monocle akan ikut pola yang sama dengan tambahan `createWindowCalls` dan `getWindowPanesCalls`.

## Out of Scope

- **Keybinding navigasi** — user handle sendiri. Tidak ada hotkey baru dari extension.
- **Rename tab berdasarkan task** — window name hanya agent type, tidak include detail task.
- **Custom window layout dalam group** — equalize sederhana (semua pane sama besar).
- **Drag & drop reorder** — monocle static, tidak ada interactive pane reordering.
- **Monocle untuk resume session** — resume tetap pake createSurface() dengan layout yang aktif.
- **Visual testing** — monocle menghasilkan pane/terminal, bukan visual web component.

## Further Notes

- Monocle memaksimalkan layar kecil dengan prinsip tab-stacking: hanya 1 sub-agent yang terlihat dalam satu waktu, full screen.
- User navigasi antar window/tab menggunakan keybinding favorit masing-masing (tmux prefix + n/p atau herdr keybinding).
- Layout default tetap **tiling** untuk backward compatibility.
- Untuk tim yang suka monocle, bisa set default di `.pi/subagent-config.json` level project.

title: Implement: bottom-stack layout + split ratio for both layouts
description:
## Parent

pi-my-subagents-17w — Configurable Pane Layout: Tiling & Bottom-Stack

## What to build

This is the main feature slice. Parameterize the pane layout system so users who set `layout: "bottom-stack"` see sub-agents arranged horizontally at the bottom, while users who set `layout: "tiling"` (or leave unset) keep the existing left-right arrangement — but now with a 70/30 split ratio.

### Bottom-stack behavior
- First sub-agent: split **down** from main (↕)
- Subsequent sub-agents: split **right** from previous sub-agent (↔)
- Equalize **widths** of all bottom-row panes (like tiling equalizes heights)
- Pane-closure fallback: split **down** (layout-aware, not hardcoded `right`)

### Split ratio (both layouts)
- `DEFAULT_SPLIT_RATIO = 0.30` — new pane gets 30%, main gets 70%
- **Tiling**: first split uses `-p 30` (tmux) / `--ratio 0.3` (herdr) so main pane gets 70% width
- **Bottom-stack**: first split uses same ratio, main gets 70% height

### Technical changes
- `createTileSurface` gains a `layoutMode: LayoutType` parameter that controls first-split direction, subsequent-split direction, and equalize dimension
- `splitFn` closure gains an optional 4th `ratio` parameter; `createSurfaceSplit` passes it as `-p` (tmux) or `--ratio` (herdr)
- New width functions:
  - **tmux**: `tmuxGetPaneWidth()` using `display -p '#{pane_width}'`, `tmuxResizeWidths()` using `resize-pane -x`
  - **herdr**: `herdrGetPaneWidth()`, `herdrResizeWidths()` using `pane resize --direction left/right`
- `createSurface()` in `mux.ts` reads config via `loadSubagentConfig()` and dispatches to the correct layout; validates layout value (fallback to `"tiling"` for invalid values)
- Pane-closure catch block uses layout-aware first-split direction
- Tests: bottom-stack split direction, width equalize, pane-closure fallback, layout reset

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Setting `layout: "bottom-stack"` spawns first sub-agent with a down split from main
- [ ] Subsequent sub-agents in bottom-stack split right from the previous sub-agent
- [ ] Bottom-stack equalises widths when a new sub-agent is added
- [ ] Bottom-stack pane-closure fallback uses down split (not right)
- [ ] Setting `layout: "tiling"` spawns sub-agents with existing right-then-down pattern, now with 70/30 ratio
- [ ] Unset/`undefined` layout defaults to `"tiling"`
- [ ] Invalid layout value falls back to `"tiling"`
- [ ] Works identically in both tmux and herdr backends
- [ ] /reload after config change picks up new layout
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
- [ ] New bottom-stack and split-ratio tests pass

## Blocked by

- `pi-my-subagents-gs3` — Refactor: rename equalizeStack/resetTilingLayout to layout-neutral names
- `pi-my-subagents-bl5` — Config & type: add LayoutType to config schema and fix merge
close_reason: Done — bottom-stack layout + split ratio, committed 25be7df

title: Config & type: add LayoutType to config schema and fix merge
description:
## Parent

pi-my-subagents-17w — Configurable Pane Layout: Tiling & Bottom-Stack

## What to build

Add the config and type infrastructure so that users can set their preferred pane layout, even though layout selection doesn't yet change behavior.

Changes:
- Add `LayoutType = "tiling" | "bottom-stack"` type to `types.ts`
- Add `layout?: LayoutType` to the `SubagentConfig` interface
- Update `loadSubagentConfig()` to merge the top-level `layout` field: `project?.layout ?? global?.layout`
- Export `loadSubagentConfig()` so `mux.ts` can consume it
- Add `layout` as an optional field in the `/subagent-config` wizard (commands + wizard UI)

The layout field isn't read by any layout code yet — that comes in a later slice. This slice only makes the config parseable and settable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `LayoutType` type exists in `types.ts`
- [ ] `SubagentConfig` has `layout?: LayoutType`
- [ ] `loadSubagentConfig()` merges `layout` from project overriding global
- [ ] `loadSubagentConfig()` is exported
- [ ] Wizard `/subagent-config` includes a layout field with options "tiling" and "bottom-stack"
- [ ] TypeScript compilation passes

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately

title: Refactor: rename equalizeStack/resetTilingLayout to layout-neutral names
description:
## Parent

pi-my-subagents-17w — Configurable Pane Layout: Tiling & Bottom-Stack

## What to build

Rename two functions in the subagent pane layout module to be layout-neutral, preparing for multiple layout modes:

- `equalizeStack` → `equalizePanes` with generic parameter names (`getSizeFn`, `targetSize` instead of `getHeightFn`, `targetHeight`)
- `resetTilingLayout` → `resetLayout`

Provide backwards-compatible aliases (`equalizeStack`, `resetTilingLayout`) so no import breaks outside the module under test.

Update test imports in `test/mux-layout.test.ts` to use the new names.

No behavioral change — existing tests pass, compilation succeeds.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `equalizePanes` exists with generic param names, `equalizeStack` is an alias
- [ ] `resetLayout` exists, `resetTilingLayout` is an alias
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compilation passes with no errors
- [ ] No behavioral change

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately

title: Configurable Pane Layout: Tiling & Bottom-Stack
description:
## Problem Statement

Currently, sub-agent panes only support a single layout: DWM-style tiling (master pane on the left, sub-agent stack on the right). Users who prefer bottom-stack layout (master on top, sub-agents spread horizontally at the bottom) must work against the default arrangement. This is purely a user preference about pane arrangement — the feature doesn't change agent behavior, just how the panes appear on screen.

The config system already supports global + per-project override for model, skills, and extensions. There is no reason layout preference should be harder to configure.

## Solution

Add a top-level `layout` field to `subagent-config.json` that accepts either `"tiling"` (existing) or `"bottom-stack"` (new). The config follows the same global/project merge pattern as existing model/skills/extensions settings.

Both layouts use a shared 30% split ratio for the sub-agent area:
- `tiling`: main agent 70% width (left), sub-agents 30% width (right stack)
- `bottom-stack`: main agent 70% height (top), sub-agents 30% height (bottom row)

### Tiling (existing)
```
┌──────────┬──────┐
│          │ sub-a│
│  main    ├──────┤
│  agent   │ sub-b│
│          ├──────┤
│          │ sub-c│
└──────────┴──────┘
```

### Bottom Stack (new)
```
┌──────────────────────┐
│                      │
│    Main Agent        │
│                      │
├──────┬──────┬───────┤
│sub-a │sub-b │ sub-c │
└──────┴──────┴───────┘
```

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want to choose between tiling and bottom-stack layouts, so that sub-agents are arranged the way I visually prefer.
2. As a user, I want the layout config to support global default + per-project override, so I can have different layouts for different projects.
3. As a user, I want sub-agents to consistently get 30% of the available space, so the main agent doesn't feel cramped regardless of layout.
4. As a user, I want the layout to work identically in both tmux and herdr backends, so my preference isn't tied to a specific terminal multiplexer.
5. As a user, I want bottom-stack sub-agents to tile horizontally across the bottom as I add them, so I can see multiple sub-agents at once.
6. As a user, I want the existing tiling layout to remain unchanged when I don't configure a layout, so my workflow isn't affected.
7. As a user, I want the bottom-stack sub-agent row to equalise widths when a new sub-agent is added, just like the tiling layout equalises heights.
8. As a user, I want pane closure (sub-agent exits) to correctly reset to the current layout's first-split direction, so the layout doesn't drift.
9. As a user, I want to be warned if I set an invalid layout value, so I don't silently get unexpected behaviour.

## Implementation Decisions

### Config schema
- `layout` is a **top-level** field in `SubagentConfig` (not per-agent), because layout is a user preference about pane arrangement, not an agent capability.
- Same global/project merge pattern as model/skills/extensions: `project?.layout ?? global?.layout ?? undefined`.
- Default (no config, or `undefined`): `"tiling"`.

### Architecture
- `mux-layout.ts` stays as **one file** — `createTileSurface` becomes parameterized with a `layoutMode: LayoutType` parameter.
- Differences between layouts are parameterized as: first-split direction, subsequent-split direction, equalize dimension (width vs height), and split ratio.
- Constants: `DEFAULT_SPLIT_RATIO = 0.30` (shared, new pane's share).

### SplitFn signature
- The `splitFn` closure gains an optional 4th `ratio` parameter: `(name, direction, fromSurface?, ratio?)`.
- `ratio` = new pane's share (0.0–1.0). Conversion to backend-specific format:
  - **tmux**: split-window `-p <Math.round((1 - ratio) * 100)>` (tmux `-p` is the existing pane's percentage)
  - **herdr**: pane split `--ratio <ratio>` (herdr `--ratio` is the new pane's share)

### Equalize function
- Rename `equalizeStack` → `equalizePanes` with generic parameter names (`getSizeFn`, `targetSize`).
- `equalizeStack` and `equalizeWidths` become aliases for backward compatibility.

### Pane-closure fallback
- Catch block in `createTileSurface` must be layout-aware: bottom-stack fallback splits `down` instead of `right`.

### Reset function
- Rename `resetTilingLayout` → `resetLayout`.

### Config merge fix
- `loadSubagentConfig()` currently only merges `agents`. Must also merge `layout: project?.layout ?? global?.layout ?? undefined`.

### Layout validation
- In `createSurface`, validate config value against `["tiling", "bottom-stack"]`. Fallback to `"tiling"` for invalid values.

### Module responsibilities
- `mux.ts:createSurface()` reads config and dispatches to correct layout function. Signature becomes `export function createSurface(name: string, layout?: LayoutType): string`.
- `spawner.ts` and `subagent.ts` callers don't need changes (layout is read internally).
- `config.ts` exports `loadSubagentConfig()` for use by `mux.ts`.

## Testing Decisions

A good test verifies external behaviour: what layout decisions the function makes (split direction, equalize dimension), not how the backends execute them.

### Test module
- `test/mux-layout.test.ts` — same seam as existing tests.

### Test cases
- Bottom-stack: first split is `down` with `DEFAULT_SPLIT_RATIO`.
- Bottom-stack: second split is `right`.
- Bottom-stack: equalize widths on 2+ sub-agents.
- Bottom-stack: pane-closure fallback is `down` with ratio.
- Tiling: unchanged behaviour (existing tests remain passing).
- Tiling: first split now includes ratio.
- `resetLayout`: clears stack regardless of layout.
- Invalid layout config: falls back to `"tiling"`.

### Prior art
- Existing tests in `test/mux-layout.test.ts` use mocked `splitFn`/`resizeFn`/`getHeightFn` with `node:test` and `node:assert/strict`. New tests follow the same pattern.

## Out of Scope

- Per-agent layout override (top-level only is sufficient for MVP).
- Additional layouts beyond `tiling` and `bottom-stack` (e.g. monocle, horizontal-stack, spiral).
- Dynamic layout switching mid-session without `/reload`.
- Interactive pane rearrangement by the user.
- Custom split ratio configuration (hardcoded at 30%, user-configurable split ratio is future work).

## Further Notes

- Both tmux and herdr backends have been verified to support the required operations:
  - tmux: `split-window -p`, `resize-pane -x` (width)
  - herdr: `pane split --ratio`, `pane resize --direction left/right`
- The reviewer confirmed architecture is "clean architectural fit" and "right level of abstraction".
- Layout changes require `/reload` on the extension config.
close_reason: Done — all 3 slices implemented, tested, and pushed

title: Sub-agent tidak aware dengan caller_ping tool — perlu promosi di system prompt / task preamble
description:
Fix implemented in spawner.ts:
- modeHint auto-exit: added caller_ping mention
- modeHint non-auto-exit: added caller_ping mention
- summaryInstruction non-auto-exit: added caller_ping mention

Files changed: pi-extension/subagents/spawner.ts

title: [Slice 3] Tmux resize stack
description:
## Parent
pi-my-subagents-v8a

## What to build
Implementasi `tmuxResizeStack(panes, targetLines)` di `tmux-mux.ts`:
```
totalHeight = tmux display-message -p -t panes[0] "#{pane_height}"
each = Math.floor(totalHeight / panes.length)
tmux resize-pane -t <pane> -y <each>
```

Manual verification (butuh tmux runtime).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tmuxResizeStack menerima array panes + target lines
- [ ] Resize tmux panes ke height yang benar
- [ ] Tidak ada error handling yang silent-fail

## Blocked by
- [Slice 0] Refactor mux.ts (pi-my-subagents-v8a.1)
- [Slice 1] Core equalize logic (pi-my-subagents-v8a.2)

comments.author: djs21

title: [Slice 2] Herdr resize stack
description:
## Parent
pi-my-subagents-v8a

## What to build
Implementasi `herdrResizeStack(panes, targetHeight)` di `herdr-mux.ts`:
```
herdr pane resize <pane> --direction up/down --amount <ratio>
```

Setelah Slice 1 menyediakan resizeFn contract, implement herdr-specific version. Manual verification (butuh herdr runtime).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] herdrResizeStack menerima array panes + target height
- [ ] Resize herdr panes ke height yang benar
- [ ] Tidak ada error handling yang silent-fail

## Blocked by
- [Slice 0] Refactor mux.ts (pi-my-subagents-v8a.1)
- [Slice 1] Core equalize logic (pi-my-subagents-v8a.2)

comments.author: djs21

title: [Slice 1] Core equalize logic — stackPanes + equalizeStack + resizeFn + mock test
description:
## Parent
pi-my-subagents-v8a

## What to build
Tambahkan equalize stack logic di `mux-layout.ts`:
- Module-level `stackPanes: string[]` untuk tracking semua pane di kanan
- `resizeFn` dependency (di-pass dari luar, mirip splitFn)
- `equalizeStack(backend, resizeFn)` dipanggil setelah tiap split-down sukses:
  * Dapatkan total height dari stackPanes[0]
  * Hitung target = totalHeight / stackPanes.length
  * Panggil resizeFn(stackPanes, target, backend)
- Reset `stackPanes = []` di `resetTilingLayout()` dan di catch block pane closure
- Update `createTileSurface` signature: tambah parameter `resizeFn`

Unit test di `test/test.ts` (pola mock pure functions):
- Test 1 subagent → split right, NO equalize
- Test 2 subagents → split right + split down → equalize 50/50
- Test 3+ subagents → split right + down + down → equalize 33/33/33
- Test pane closure → reset stack, fallback right split
- Test resetTilingLayout() → stack kosong

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] stackPanes tercatat dengan benar setelah tiap split
- [ ] equalizeStack dipanggil setelah tiap split-down sukses
- [ ] resizeFn menerima daftar panes yang benar
- [ ] resetTilingLayout() reset stackPanes
- [ ] Pane closure reset stackPanes
- [ ] Semua unit test passing

## Blocked by
- pi-my-subagents-v8a (PRD)
close_reason: ✅ mux-layout.ts — equalizeStack, stackPanes, 5 TDD tests passing

comments.author: djs21
comments.text:
## TDD Plan — Slice 1: Core Equalize Logic

### Goal
Add equalize stack logic to mux-layout.ts with 5 TDD cycles (one test at a time, vertical tracer bullets).

### Files Modified
- `pi-extension/subagents/mux-layout.ts` — add stackPanes, equalizeStack(), update createTileSurface signature
- `test/test.ts` — add describe block for equalize stack tests

### Key Contracts

```
// resizeFn
(panes: string[], targetHeight: number, backend: MuxBackend | null) => void

// getHeightFn
(pane: string, backend: MuxBackend | null) => number

// equalizeStack (exported)
export function equalizeStack(
  panes: string[],
  backend: MuxBackend | null,
  resizeFn: (panes: string[], targetHeight: number, backend: MuxBackend | null) => void,
  getHeightFn: (pane: string, backend: MuxBackend | null) => number,
): void
```

### TDD Cycle 1: Single subagent — no equalize
RED: Test that createTileSurface with 1 subagent calls splitFn("right") and does NOT call resizeFn.
GREEN: Add resizeFn and getHeightFn params to createTileSurface. Don't call equalizeStack yet.

### TDD Cycle 2: Two subagents — equalize 50/50
RED: Test that 2nd subagent triggers equalize with targetHeight=300 (totalHeight=600/2).
GREEN: Add stackPanes tracking + equalizeStack with height calc.

### TDD Cycle 3: Three subagents — equalize 33/33/33
RED: Test that 3rd subagent triggers equalize with targetHeight=200 (totalHeight=600/3).
GREEN: Should pass without new code — validates algorithm generalizes.

### TDD Cycle 4: Pane closure — reset stack
RED: Test that pane closure resets stackPanes and fallback right split starts fresh.
GREEN: Add stackPanes=[] in catch block + set stackPanes=[newPane] after fallback.

### TDD Cycle 5: resetTilingLayout — stack kosong
RED: Test that resetTilingLayout() clears stackPanes.
GREEN: Add stackPanes=[] in resetTilingLayout().

### Mock Pattern (from existing test.ts)
```
function mockSplitFn(name, direction, from?) { /* record call, return pane-id */ }
function mockResizeFn(panes, targetHeight, backend) { /* record call */ }
function mockGetHeightFn(pane, backend) { return heightReturns[pane] ?? 0; }
```

### Verification
- All 5 test cycles passing
- resizeFn receives correct pane list and Math.floor(totalHeight / N) targetHeight

title: [Slice 0] Refactor mux.ts — pisah backend-specific code
description:
## Parent
pi-my-subagents-v8a

## What to build
Refactor `mux.ts` jadi 3 file:
- `mux-common.ts` — shared: pollForExit, sendLongCommand, shellEscape, readScreen, MuxBackend type, isMuxAvailable, logToSurface, sendInput, sendKeys, createSurface
- `herdr-mux.ts` — herdrSplitSurface, herdrResizeStack, herdrGetPaneHeight
- `tmux-mux.ts` — tmuxSplitSurface, tmuxResizeStack, tmuxGetPaneHeight

`mux.ts` tetap sebagai entry point yang import dari ketiga file dan re-export semua symbol.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Semua symbol yang ada di mux.ts tetap bisa di-import dari mux.ts (backward compat)
- [ ] herdr-mux.ts dan tmux-mux.ts masing-masing hanya berisi backend-specific code
- [ ] mux-common.ts berisi shared utilities
- [ ] Semua import yang reference mux.ts di codebase lain tetap jalan
- [ ] Module yang ada test tetap passing

## Blocked by
None — can start immediately
close_reason: Tidak diperlukan — resize backends di file terpisah (herdr-mux.ts, tmux-mux.ts, mux-layout.ts), mux.ts tetap sebagai entry point dengan wiring minimal. Pendekatan file baru lebih bersih daripada refactor besar-besaran.

comments.author: djs21
comments.text:
## TDD Plan — Slice 0: Refactor mux.ts

### Goal
Split mux.ts into 3 files for separation of concerns. Backward compatible — all imports from mux.ts still work.

### File Structure
- `mux-common.ts` — shared: pollForExit, sendLongCommand, shellEscape, readScreen, readScreenAsync, MuxBackend type, getMuxBackend, isMuxAvailable, createSurface, sendCommand, sendEscape, closeSurface, renameCurrentTab, renameWorkspace, isFishShell, exitStatusVar
- `herdr-mux.ts` — herdrSplitSurface (from createSurfaceSplit herdr branch), herdrResizeStack (stub), herdrGetPaneHeight (stub)
- `tmux-mux.ts` — tmuxSplitSurface (from createSurfaceSplit tmux branch), tmuxResizeStack (stub), tmuxGetPaneHeight (stub)
- `mux.ts` — re-exports all symbols from the 3 files

### Steps
1. Create mux-common.ts — extract shared code from mux.ts
2. Create herdr-mux.ts — extract herdr-specific code + stubs for resize/height
3. Create tmux-mux.ts — extract tmux-specific code + stubs for resize/height
4. Update mux.ts — import and re-export from 3 files
5. Run `npm test` — all existing tests must pass
6. Verify all imports from mux.ts across codebase still work

### Verification
- All symbols importable from mux.ts (backward compat)
- npm test passes
- No changes needed in other modules

title: PRD: Equalize Stack — DWM-style subagent pane equal height
description:
## PRD: Equalize Stack — DWM-style subagent pane equal height

### Problem
Sequential 50/50 splits of subagent panes produce unequal heights, making the layout look uneven.

### Solution
Add equalizeStack function that calculates totalHeight/N for each pane and calls resizeFn. Track stackPanes in mux-layout.ts. Implement per-backend resize backends in herdr-mux.ts (ratio-delta via layout JSON) and tmux-mux.ts (absolute height via resize-pane -y).

### Implementation Status: ✅ COMPLETE

- Slice 1 (core equalize logic): mux-layout.ts — equalizeStack, stackPanes, 5 TDD tests ✅
- Slice 2 (herdr resize): herdr-mux.ts — herdrResizeStack, herdrGetPaneHeight ✅
- Slice 3 (tmux resize): tmux-mux.ts — tmuxResizeStack, tmuxGetPaneHeight ✅
- Wiring: mux.ts:createSurface() passes resizeFn/getHeightFn to createTileSurface ✅

### Files
- pi-extension/subagents/mux-layout.ts (modified)
- pi-extension/subagents/herdr-mux.ts (new)
- pi-extension/subagents/tmux-mux.ts (new)
- pi-extension/subagents/mux.ts (modified)
- test/mux-layout.test.ts (new — 5 TDD tests)
- pi-extension/subagents/spawner.ts (modified — caller_ping)
- AGENTS.md, .gitignore (updated)
